prompts.text: {player1}, welcome to the wild side! Take 2 sips to kick things off.
{player1}, give out 3 sips -- you're the alpha of the pack tonight.
{player1}, take 3 sips. Nature is brutal and so is this game.
{player1}, give out 2 sips to whoever would die first in the wilderness.
{player1}, take 2 sips. Touch grass? Tonight you're drinking grass.
{player1}, hand out 2 sips to whoever is most afraid of bugs.
First to shout it out: What animal has fingerprints so similar to humans that they've confused crime scene investigators? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Koalas -- their fingerprints are virtually indistinguishable from human fingerprints under a microscope

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the fastest land animal? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The cheetah -- up to 70 mph in short bursts. But it overheats after about 30 seconds and has to stop.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What animal sleeps the most hours per day? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The koala -- up to 22 hours a day. Eucalyptus leaves are so low in nutrition they barely have energy to stay awake.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the only mammal that can truly fly? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Bats -- flying squirrels and colugos glide, but bats have powered flight like birds

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How long does it take a sloth to digest a single meal? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Up to 30 days -- they have the slowest metabolism of any non-hibernating mammal. They only poop once a week.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What percentage of a panda's day is spent eating? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: About 50% -- they eat 26-84 pounds of bamboo daily because it's so low in nutrients. They still have a carnivore's digestive system.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What do elephants do when they encounter the bones of a dead elephant? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: They gently touch and examine the bones with their trunks, sometimes lingering for hours -- they're one of the few animals that seem to mourn their dead

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How fast can a hippo run on land? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Up to 20 mph -- faster than most humans. Despite weighing up to 4,000 pounds, they can outrun you.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What animal has a tongue so long it can clean its own ears? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The giraffe -- their tongues are 18-20 inches long and are dark purple/black to prevent sunburn

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the loudest animal on land? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The howler monkey -- its call can be heard up to 3 miles away through dense forest

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the largest rodent in the world? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The capybara -- up to 140 pounds. They're basically giant guinea pigs that can hold their breath underwater for 5 minutes.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What mammal lays eggs? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The platypus (and echidnas) -- the only egg-laying mammals. Male platypuses are also venomous, with spurs on their hind legs.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What animal can survive being frozen solid and then thawed? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The wood frog -- in winter, up to 65% of its body can freeze. Its heart stops, it stops breathing, and it thaws in spring completely fine.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Why do wombat droppings come out cube-shaped? Winner gives out 4 sips.
prompts.answer: Their intestines have varying elasticity that shapes the poop into cubes -- it helps them mark territory on rocks and logs without the poop rolling away

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the only animal whose males give birth? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The seahorse -- males have a brood pouch where they carry and deliver up to 2,000 babies at once

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What bird can fly backwards? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The hummingbird -- they're the only birds that can sustain hovering and fly in any direction, including backwards and upside down

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How fast does a hummingbird's heart beat? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Up to 1,200 beats per minute -- and they visit 1,000-2,000 flowers per day

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What bird has the largest wingspan of any living bird? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The wandering albatross -- up to 11.5 feet. They can fly thousands of miles without flapping their wings using dynamic soaring.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What bird can mimic chainsaws, camera shutters, and car alarms? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The lyrebird -- Australian birds that can perfectly imitate virtually any sound they hear, including human speech and machinery

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the fastest bird in the world during a dive? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The peregrine falcon -- over 240 mph in a stoop (hunting dive). It's the fastest animal on Earth.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What bird uses tools like sticks to fish for insects? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The New Caledonian crow -- they manufacture and use tools, and can solve multi-step puzzles. They're considered one of the smartest animals on Earth.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What bird dances to attract a mate by moonwalking? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The red-capped manakin -- the male does a rapid backward slide along a branch that looks exactly like Michael Jackson's moonwalk

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the largest bird egg relative to the mother's body size? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The kiwi -- its egg is about 20% of its body weight. That's like a human giving birth to a 4-year-old.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What bird species decorates its nest with colorful objects to attract females? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The bowerbird -- males build elaborate structures decorated with flowers, shells, and even stolen human objects sorted by color

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Can penguins taste food? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Barely -- they've lost the ability to taste sweet, bitter, and umami. They can only detect salty and sour. They swallow fish whole anyway.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What bird can sleep while flying? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The common swift -- they can spend 10 months in the air without landing, sleeping in short bursts while gliding

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many times per second does a housefly flap its wings? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: About 200 times per second -- which is what creates the buzzing sound

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What insect can carry 50 times its own body weight? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The ant -- some species can carry up to 50x their body weight. A leafcutter ant colony can strip a tree bare overnight.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many eyes does a spider typically have? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 8 -- though some species have 6, 4, 2, or even zero (cave spiders). The jumping spider has the best vision of any spider.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What insect has been on Earth for over 300 million years -- before dinosaurs existed? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Cockroaches -- and they can survive a week without their head, only dying because they can't drink water

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How far can a flea jump relative to its body size? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Up to 150 times its own body length -- equivalent to a human jumping over a 75-story building

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What spider's bite can cause an erection lasting hours in men? Winner gives out 4 sips.
prompts.answer: The Brazilian wandering spider (Phoneutria) -- scientists have actually studied its venom for potential erectile dysfunction treatment

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What insect pollinates more than 80% of all flowering plants? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Bees -- without them, about one-third of the food we eat would disappear

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many individual bees are in a single honeybee hive? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 20,000 to 80,000 -- and they communicate the location of flowers through a 'waggle dance'

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What creature kills more humans per year than any other animal? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: The mosquito -- over 700,000 human deaths per year through diseases like malaria, dengue, and Zika

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What happens when a queen bee mates? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: She takes a single 'mating flight,' mates with 10-20 drones mid-air, stores all the sperm she'll ever need, and never mates again -- she can lay 2,000 eggs per day for years.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the largest insect in the world by weight? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The giant weta from New Zealand -- it can weigh over 2.5 ounces, heavier than a sparrow

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How long have dragonflies existed on Earth? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Over 300 million years -- prehistoric dragonflies had wingspans of over 2 feet. Modern dragonflies catch 95% of the prey they chase.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What insect literally explodes to defend its colony? Winner gives out 4 sips.
prompts.answer: The Malaysian exploding ant (Colobopsis explodens) -- worker ants rupture their own bodies to spray toxic goo on predators

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What spider eats its mother alive as its first meal? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The matriphagy spider -- the mother literally lets her babies eat her alive. Several spider species do this.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many insects are estimated to exist for every single human on Earth? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: About 1.4 billion -- at any given time, there are roughly 10 quintillion insects alive on Earth

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What reptile can detach its own tail to escape predators? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Many lizards, especially geckos -- the tail keeps wriggling to distract the predator while the lizard escapes. It grows back.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How long can a crocodile go without eating? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Up to 2 years -- saltwater crocs are incredibly efficient and can slow their metabolism to near-zero

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What determines whether a sea turtle egg becomes male or female? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The temperature of the sand -- warmer nests produce females, cooler nests produce males. Climate change is making almost all new turtles female.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What snake can fly (glide) between trees? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The paradise tree snake -- it launches itself from branches and flattens its body to glide up to 30 feet through the air

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the most venomous snake on land? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The inland taipan -- one bite has enough venom to kill 100 adult humans. Despite this, no human deaths have been recorded because it's incredibly shy and lives in remote Australia.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Can a headless snake still bite? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Yes -- a severed snake head can still deliver a venomous bite for up to an hour after being cut off due to reflexes

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What frog is so poisonous that indigenous people use it on blow darts? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The golden poison dart frog -- one frog has enough poison to kill 10 adult men. It gets its toxicity from the insects it eats.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How old is the oldest known tortoise to have ever lived? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Jonathan the Seychelles giant tortoise -- born around 1832, he's over 190 years old and still alive. He's the oldest known living land animal.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What lizard can run on water? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The basilisk lizard (nicknamed the 'Jesus Christ lizard') -- it runs on its hind legs so fast it can sprint across water surfaces

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What reptile has a third eye on top of its head? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The tuatara -- a reptile from New Zealand. The third 'parietal eye' has a lens and retina but is covered by scales. It detects light and helps regulate body rhythms.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many hearts does a cockroach have? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 13 chambers in its tubular heart -- not technically separate hearts but a multi-chambered system

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the oldest living organism on Earth? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A colony of quaking aspen trees called 'Pando' in Utah -- the root system is roughly 80,000 years old and is technically one organism

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What plant can grow up to 35 inches in a single day? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Bamboo -- some species grow over 35 inches per day. You can literally watch it grow in real time.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the largest living tree on Earth by volume? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: General Sherman, a giant sequoia in California -- 52,500 cubic feet of wood. It weighs an estimated 2.7 million pounds.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What flower smells like rotting flesh and can be over 10 feet tall? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The corpse flower (Amorphophallus titanum) -- it only blooms every 7-10 years and the stench attracts pollinating beetles and flies

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What plant can 'count'? Winner gives out 4 sips.
prompts.answer: The Venus flytrap -- it counts the number of times trigger hairs are touched. One touch = alert, two touches within 20 seconds = snap shut. It can count to five to decide how much digestive enzyme to release.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What tree produces a fruit that kills more people per year than sharks? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The coconut palm -- falling coconuts kill about 150 people per year compared to about 5 shark fatalities

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How do trees in a forest communicate with each other? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Through an underground fungal network nicknamed the 'Wood Wide Web' -- mycorrhizal fungi connect tree roots and allow them to share nutrients and even send chemical warning signals about pests

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What plant has seeds that can only germinate after a forest fire? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Many pine species (like the lodgepole pine) have serotinous cones that are sealed with resin and only open when heated by fire -- it's an adaptation to fire-prone ecosystems

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the largest flower in the world? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Rafflesia arnoldii -- up to 3 feet across and weighing 24 pounds. It's a parasite with no roots, stems, or leaves, and it smells like rotting meat.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What color were carrots before the 17th century? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Purple (and white/yellow) -- orange carrots were bred by the Dutch in the 17th century, possibly to honor William of Orange

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the hottest temperature ever recorded on Earth? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 134 degrees F (56.7 degrees C) in Death Valley, California in 1913

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How hot can a lightning bolt get? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Up to 30,000 Kelvin (53,540 degrees F) -- that's 5 times hotter than the surface of the sun

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What natural disaster can a volcanic eruption trigger besides lava flows? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Tsunamis, earthquakes, mudflows (lahars), lightning storms, acid rain, and even temporary global cooling from ash blocking sunlight

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the driest place on Earth? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Atacama Desert in Chile -- some weather stations there have NEVER recorded rain. Parts have been dry for 40 million years.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is a firenado? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A fire tornado -- a vortex of flame that forms when intense heat and wind combine during wildfires. They can reach temperatures of 2,000 degrees F and wind speeds of 140 mph.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is ball lightning? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A rare, unexplained phenomenon where a glowing sphere of light floats through the air during a thunderstorm. Science still can't fully explain it.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How fast can a tsunami travel across the open ocean? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Up to 500 mph -- as fast as a jet plane. In deep water it's barely noticeable, but it slows and grows tall as it hits shallow water.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the wettest place on Earth? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Mawsynram, India -- averages about 467 inches of rain per year (about 39 feet)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What causes the Northern Lights (aurora borealis)? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Charged particles from the sun colliding with gases in Earth's atmosphere -- different gases produce different colors (oxygen = green/red, nitrogen = blue/purple)

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the loudest sound in recorded history? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The eruption of Krakatoa in 1883 -- it was heard 3,000 miles away and ruptured the eardrums of people 40 miles away. The pressure wave circled the Earth 4 times.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What creates the sound of thunder? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The rapid heating and expansion of air around a lightning bolt -- the air expands faster than the speed of sound, creating a shockwave

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is a derecho? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A widespread, long-lived straight-line windstorm associated with a fast-moving band of severe thunderstorms. Wind can exceed 100 mph along a path 250+ miles wide.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How old is the Earth? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: About 4.54 billion years old

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What percentage of Earth's water is freshwater? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: About 3% -- and most of that is locked in ice caps and glaciers. Less than 1% is accessible for human use.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the Ring of Fire? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: A horseshoe-shaped zone around the Pacific Ocean where about 75% of the world's volcanoes are located and about 90% of earthquakes occur

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What makes the Dead Sea so salty? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Water flows in but can't flow out (no outlet) -- it only loses water through evaporation, leaving the salt behind. It's about 10 times saltier than the ocean.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the largest desert on Earth? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Antarctica -- deserts are defined by precipitation, not temperature. Antarctica gets less than 10 inches of precipitation per year.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What causes quicksand, and can it actually swallow you? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Waterlogged sand that can't support weight. But no -- you can't actually sink fully. The human body is less dense than quicksand. You'd sink to about your waist.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How much of Earth's oxygen is produced by the ocean? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: 50-80% -- mostly from phytoplankton, not rainforests. The Amazon produces about 6% of Earth's oxygen.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What are the oldest known fossils on Earth? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Stromatolites -- fossilized microbial mats about 3.5 billion years old found in Western Australia

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the deepest hole ever dug by humans? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Kola Superdeep Borehole in Russia -- 40,230 feet (7.6 miles) deep. They stopped because the temperature reached 356 degrees F, too hot for the drill to function.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How fast is the Earth spinning at the equator? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: About 1,040 mph -- you don't feel it because everything around you (including the atmosphere) is moving at the same speed

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What happens during a 'zombie ant' infection? Winner gives out 4 sips.
prompts.answer: The Ophiocordyceps fungus infects an ant's brain, controls its behavior, forces it to climb a plant and clamp down, then a fungal stalk erupts from its head to spread spores. It literally mind-controls the ant.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What animal can survive in the vacuum of outer space? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The tardigrade (water bear) -- these microscopic animals can survive extreme temperatures, radiation, the vacuum of space, and pressures 6x the deepest ocean trench

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What percentage of all species that have ever lived on Earth are now extinct? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Over 99% -- extinction is the norm, not the exception. We're currently in what scientists call the 6th mass extinction.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What natural phenomenon can make rocks move across a flat desert by themselves? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Sailing stones in Death Valley -- thin ice sheets form under the rocks, and wind pushes the ice-encased rocks across the mud, leaving tracks

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the 'blob' that was found pulsating on the ocean floor in the 1990s? Winner gives out 4 sips.
prompts.answer: A pyrosome -- a colony of thousands of tiny organisms (tunicates) that form a giant hollow tube. They can be 60 feet long and glow bioluminescently.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What bird species goes to 'war' with coordinated battle tactics? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Crows and ravens -- they organize group attacks, post sentinels, and even hold 'funerals' where they gather silently around a dead crow

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What happens to a caterpillar inside a chrysalis? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: It completely dissolves into liquid goo (except for special clusters of cells called imaginal discs) and then reassembles into a butterfly. It's not a makeover -- it's a full liquefaction and rebuild.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What animal has the most complex eyes in the animal kingdom? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The mantis shrimp -- it has 16 types of color receptors (humans have 3). They can see UV light, polarized light, and possibly even circular polarized light. They also punch so fast they boil the water around their fists.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What tree is called the 'Tree of Death' because everything about it is toxic? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The manchineel tree -- its fruit, sap, bark, and even the smoke from burning it are toxic. Rainwater dripping off its leaves can burn your skin.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What happens when you put a dead whale's body under a microscope years after it sank to the ocean floor? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: You find 'whale fall' ecosystems -- entire communities of specialized organisms that can sustain themselves for 50-100 years on a single whale carcass

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What creature has the most powerful punch in the animal kingdom? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The mantis shrimp -- its punch accelerates at the same force as a .22 caliber bullet and creates a flash of light and heat through cavitation. It can break aquarium glass.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What percentage of the Earth's surface is covered by ocean? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: About 71% -- and the ocean contains about 97% of all water on Earth

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What animal can regenerate its entire body from a tiny piece? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The planarian flatworm -- cut one into 279 pieces and each piece regenerates into a complete worm. Some even retain memories after being decapitated and regrowing their head.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the most radiation-resistant organism on Earth? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Deinococcus radiodurans (nicknamed 'Conan the Bacterium') -- it can survive radiation doses 1,500 times what would kill a human

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What animal produces the strongest natural material on Earth? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Limpets (sea snails) -- their teeth are made of the strongest biological material ever tested, stronger than spider silk and Kevlar

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How does the horned lizard defend itself? Winner gives out 4 sips.
prompts.answer: It shoots blood from its eyes -- up to 5 feet. The blood contains a chemical that's noxious to predators, especially canines.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What animal freezes its own blood every winter to survive? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The painted turtle hatchling -- they spend their first winter frozen in their nest, with ice crystals forming between their cells. They thaw in spring and dig out.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What fish can walk on land and survive out of water for days? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The mudskipper -- it uses its pectoral fins like legs, breathes through its skin, and can even climb trees

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How many trees are on Earth -- more than the stars in the Milky Way, or fewer? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: More -- Earth has about 3 trillion trees vs. roughly 100-400 billion stars in the Milky Way

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What was the first living creature sent into space? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Fruit flies -- launched by the US in 1947 on a V-2 rocket. They survived. Laika the dog was the first animal to orbit Earth in 1957 (but didn't survive).

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What happens to plants in zero gravity? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Their roots grow in random directions since there's no gravity to guide them -- but they still respond to light (phototropism) and grow toward it

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the loudest animal on Earth (land or sea)? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The sperm whale -- its clicks reach 230 decibels, louder than a jet engine. Loud enough to vibrate a human body to death at close range.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What animal can survive without its head for weeks? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: A cockroach -- it breathes through holes in its body segments and only dies because it can't drink water. The head also survives for hours, waving its antennae.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the most abundant animal on Earth? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Antarctic krill -- estimated biomass of 500 million tons. More krill by weight than humans on Earth.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What animal has the longest migration of any creature? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The Arctic tern -- it migrates from Arctic to Antarctic and back every year, covering about 44,000 miles. Over its lifetime, that's equivalent to 3 round trips to the Moon.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the smallest mammal in the world? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The bumblebee bat (Kitti's hog-nosed bat) -- it weighs about 2 grams, roughly the weight of a penny

prompts.text: First to shout it out: How old can a Greenland shark live to be? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Over 400 years -- they're the longest-lived vertebrate. They don't reach sexual maturity until about 150 years old.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What animal has the highest blood pressure of any animal? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The giraffe -- about 280/180 mmHg (humans average 120/80). They need it to pump blood up their 6-foot necks to their brain.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What bird builds the largest nest of any animal? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The sociable weaver bird of Southern Africa -- their communal nests can be 20 feet tall, 25 feet wide, house over 100 families, and last 100+ years. They're the largest structures built by any bird.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What animal has the most teeth? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The garden snail -- it has about 20,000 teeth on a ribbon-like structure called a radula

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What color is a polar bear's skin under its white fur? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Black -- and the fur isn't actually white, it's transparent and hollow. It appears white because it reflects light.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What animal can change its biological sex? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Clownfish -- they live in groups where the largest is female. If she dies, the dominant male changes sex to become the new female. So in Finding Nemo, Marlin would've become Nemo's mom.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What happens if you cut a starfish in half? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Each half can regenerate into a complete new starfish -- some species can regrow from a single arm as long as part of the central disc is attached

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What percentage of a jellyfish is water? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: About 95% -- they have no brain, no heart, no blood, and no bones

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What animal never sleeps? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The bullfrog -- studies show they never enter a state that could be classified as sleep, though they do rest. Some scientists debate this.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What gives flamingos their pink color? Winner gives out 2 sips.
prompts.answer: Their diet -- specifically carotenoid pigments from the shrimp and algae they eat. Baby flamingos are born grey/white.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What percentage of a cat's bones are in its tail? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: About 10% -- cats have roughly 230 bones, and 19-23 of them are in the tail

prompts.text: First to shout it out: Can a cucumber become so stressed it screams? Winner gives out 4 sips.
prompts.answer: Plants actually do emit ultrasonic clicks when stressed (like when dehydrated or cut) -- researchers at Tel Aviv University recorded them in 2023. Humans can't hear them, but some animals might.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What causes glowing waves at the beach at night? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: Bioluminescent dinoflagellates -- single-celled organisms that flash blue light when disturbed by waves. It's basically nature's own glow stick.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What parasite lives inside a cricket, mind-controls it into jumping into water, then bursts out? Winner gives out 4 sips.
prompts.answer: The horsehair worm (Nematomorpha) -- it grows inside the cricket, then releases chemicals that override the cricket's survival instincts, making it jump into water where the worm can exit and reproduce

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What bird lays its eggs in other birds' nests and tricks them into raising its babies? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The cuckoo -- its eggs even mimic the host bird's eggs. When the cuckoo chick hatches, it pushes the other eggs out of the nest.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What parasitic fly turns fire ants into zombies? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The phorid fly -- it lays an egg inside the ant's body. The larva migrates to the head, eats the brain, and the head falls off. The fly emerges from the decapitated head.

prompts.text: First to shout it out: What is the largest parasite that can live inside a human body? Winner gives out 3 sips.
prompts.answer: The broad fish tapeworm -- it can grow up to 30 feet long inside human intestines

{player1} is the Apex Predator. For the next {rule_turns} rounds, anyone who makes eye contact with them takes 1 sip.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1} is no longer the Apex Predator. The jungle is safe again.

prompts.text: No one can say any animal name for the next {rule_turns} rounds. Say one? Take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Animals can be named again. The zoo is open.

prompts.text: {player1} makes the rule of the jungle. Everyone must follow it for {rule_turns} rounds. Break it = 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s jungle rule is over. Freedom.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, everyone must growl before they drink. Forget = 2 extra sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: No more growling. You're civilized again.

prompts.text: Everyone must call {player1} 'Alpha' for the next {rule_turns} rounds. Forget = 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1} is no longer the Alpha. Back to being regular.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, last person to put their hand flat on the table when someone says 'earthquake' takes 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: The earthquake rule is over. Solid ground.

prompts.text: Thumbmaster! {player1} is the Thumbmaster. Anytime they put their thumb on the table, last to notice takes 2 sips. Lasts {rule_turns} rounds.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1} is no longer the Thumbmaster.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, you must name an animal before taking any sip. Can't think of one? Double your sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: The animal-naming rule is over. Drink in peace.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, no one can point with their fingers -- use elbows only. Forget = 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Pointing with fingers is allowed again. Elbows down.

prompts.text: {player1} picks someone to be their 'pack mate.' They drink together for the next {rule_turns} rounds -- whenever one drinks, both drink.
prompts.rule_end_text: The pack is disbanded. Drink alone again.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, anyone who checks their phone takes 2 sips. We're connecting with nature tonight.
prompts.rule_end_text: Phone rule is over. Screen time is back.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, no one can say 'drink' or 'sip.' Say it? Take 3 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: You can say 'drink' again. Language restored.

prompts.text: The floor is lava for the next {rule_turns} rounds. Last person to lift their feet when someone yells 'LAVA' takes 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: The floor is no longer lava. Safe to touch ground.

prompts.text: {player1} is the Zookeeper. For the next {rule_turns} rounds, they assign an animal to each person. You must make that animal's sound before you drink.
prompts.rule_end_text: {player1}'s zoo is closed. No more animal sounds.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, everyone must whisper. Anyone who speaks at normal volume takes 2 sips. We're on a nature observation.
prompts.rule_end_text: Nature observation over. You can be loud again.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, left hand only for drinking. Use your right hand? Take 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Left-hand rule is over. Drink however you want.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, before anyone drinks, the whole group must say 'Cheers to Mother Nature.' Forget = 2 extra sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: No more toasting Mother Nature. She's had enough.

prompts.text: Evolution mode! For the next {rule_turns} rounds, {player1} assigns everyone a rank (predator/prey). Prey drink double, predators give out double.
prompts.rule_end_text: Evolution mode is over. Everyone is equal again.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, anyone who crosses their arms takes 2 sips. Arms uncrossed -- like an open field.
prompts.rule_end_text: Arm-crossing is allowed again. Cozy up.

prompts.text: For the next {rule_turns} rounds, no one can use anyone's real name. Use nature nicknames only (like 'Eagle' or 'Fern'). Slip up = 2 sips.
prompts.rule_end_text: Real names are back. The forest aliases are retired.
